rag_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope
Place implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope in AGREE annex blocks
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, and connections in the AADL model
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Write output dependency guarantees in component type annexes that relate visible outputs to visible inputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not delete generated clauses without diagnostic context unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
